string_builtins.description: FreeMarker string built-ins provide powerful string manipulation capabilities applied using the ?suffix syntax on string expressions. They cover case conversion, trimming, searching, replacing, splitting, and formatting — enabling rich text processing directly within templates without Java code.

length.description: Returns the number of characters in the string as an integer value.

upper_case.description: Converts all characters in the string to uppercase according to the current locale settings.

lower_case.description: Converts all characters in the string to lowercase according to the current locale settings.

cap_first.description: Capitalizes the first letter of the string while leaving the rest unchanged.

trim.description: Removes leading and trailing whitespace characters from the string.

contains.description: Returns true if the string contains the specified substring anywhere within it.

replace.description: Replaces all occurrences of the target substring with the replacement string.

split.description: Splits the string at each occurrence of the separator and returns a sequence of substrings.

sequence_builtins.description: FreeMarker sequence built-ins operate on ordered collections (arrays/lists) and provide operations for querying size, sorting, reversing, joining, chunking, and filtering sequences. Sequences are zero-indexed and support random access by index.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the sequence.

sort.description: Returns a new sequence with elements sorted in ascending order using natural ordering.

sort_by.description: Sorts a sequence of hashes by the specified key name, returning a new sorted sequence.

reverse.description: Returns a new sequence with elements in reverse order.

first.description: Returns the first element of the sequence, or is missing if the sequence is empty.

last.description: Returns the last element of the sequence.

join.description: Concatenates all sequence elements into a single string separated by the given separator.

chunk.description: Splits the sequence into sub-sequences of the given maximum size, useful for creating rows/columns in tables.

hash_builtins.description: FreeMarker hash built-ins operate on hash (map/dictionary) values and provide access to the keys and values collections. Hashes in FreeMarker map string keys to arbitrary values and are the primary structure for data model objects passed from the Java backend.

keys.description: Returns a sequence of all key names in the hash.

values.description: Returns a sequence of all values in the hash, in the same order as their corresponding keys.

number_builtins.description: FreeMarker number built-ins provide formatting and conversion operations for numeric values including rounding, absolute value, and string conversion with locale-aware formatting. Numbers in FreeMarker automatically handle both integers and floating-point values.

string.description: Converts the number to a formatted string; format can be 'computer' for locale-independent, 'currency', 'percent', or a Java DecimalFormat pattern.

abs.description: Returns the absolute (non-negative) value of the number.

round.description: Rounds the number to the nearest integer using half-up rounding.

floor.description: Rounds the number down to the nearest integer (toward negative infinity).

ceiling.description: Rounds the number up to the nearest integer (toward positive infinity).

if_directive.description: The conditional directive that renders its enclosed content only when the condition evaluates to true. Supports optional elseif and else branches for multi-way branching. Conditions can use comparison operators (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=), logical operators (&&, ||, !), and built-in tests.

list_directive.description: Iterates over a sequence or collection, rendering the body once for each element. The item variable holds the current element. Supports <#sep> for inter-element separators, <#items> for explicit item blocks, and <#else> for empty collection handling. Tracks iteration via item?index, item?counter, and item?is_last.

assign_directive.description: Creates or replaces a variable in the current template namespace. The simple form assigns an expression value directly. The capture form captures all output between the tags as a string value, useful for building complex strings from template output.

macro_directive.description: Defines a reusable template fragment (macro) with optional parameters and default values. Macros are called with the <@name> syntax and support nested content via <#nested>. They enable DRY template design by encapsulating repeated layout patterns.

if.description: Conditional directive. Evaluates and renders content if the condition is true.

else.description: Else branch of an if directive.

elseif.description: Else-if branch of an if directive.

list.description: Iterates over a sequence (array/collection). The most commonly used looping directive.

assign.description: Creates or replaces a variable in the current namespace.

include.description: Processes a specified template and includes the output.

import.description: Imports a library and assigns it to a namespace variable.

macro.description: Defines a macro (reusable template fragment) with parameters.

function.description: Defines a function that returns a value.

switch.description: Multi-branch selection based on value comparison.

attempt.description: Error handling. Renders recover block if attempt fails.

setting.description: Sets FreeMarker engine settings (locale, number_format, date_format, etc.).

interpolation.description: Outputs the value of an expression. The primary output mechanism.

?string.description: Converts a value to a string representation.

?number.description: Converts a string to a number.

?size.description: Returns the number of elements in a sequence or hash.

?has_content.description: Returns true if the value is not null, not empty, and not a blank string.

?upper_case.description: Converts the string to upper case.

?lower_case.description: Converts the string to lower case.

?replace.description: Replaces all occurrences of a substring.

?split.description: Splits a string into a sequence by separator.

?starts_with.description: Returns true if the string starts with the given prefix.

?contains.description: Returns true if the string contains the given substring.

?index_of.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of substring, or -1.

?length.description: Returns the length of the string.

?first.description: Returns the first element of a sequence.

?last.description: Returns the last element of a sequence.

?sort_by.description: Returns the sequence sorted by the specified sub-variable.

?filter.description: Returns a new sequence containing only elements for which the condition is true.

?map.description: Returns a new sequence where each element is the result of the expression.

?join.description: Concatenates elements of a sequence into a string with the given separator.

?keys.description: Returns a sequence containing all keys of the hash.

?values.description: Returns a sequence containing all values of the hash.

?date.description: Converts a value to a date.

?datetime.description: Converts a value to a date-time.

?j_string.description: Escapes the string for use in a Java or JavaScript string literal.

?json_string.description: Escapes the string for use in a JSON string value.

?is_string.description: Returns true if the value is a string.

?is_number.description: Returns true if the value is a number.

?is_boolean.description: Returns true if the value is a boolean.

?is_sequence.description: Returns true if the value is a sequence.

?is_hash.description: Returns true if the value is a hash.

??.description: Missing value test operator. Returns true if the variable exists and is not null.

!.description: Default value operator. Returns default if value is missing.
